run_summary: CRSP re-flagged as OVERDONE conf 3 after initial UNDERDONE entry 4 days ago. The flag's thesis has evolved: original entry cited sector rally + governance 8-K (routine); new flag cites -8.97% single-day drop on same 8-K items during risk-off tape, arguing the move is excessive for annual-meeting housekeeping. Thesis status is intact but the new flag data (5-day move -7.73%, volume 1.31x) and intra-position conflict require reassessment. MANH re-entered same day it was exited at a loss — confidence 3 fails re-entry bar (needs conf 4 + new information). Five new flags evaluated: ONTO (RATIONAL, conf 3, skip — pure sector beta), FIVE (RATIONAL, conf 4, skip — justified earnings miss, no fade edge), ALK (UNDERDONE, conf 3, exploratory — officer departure + Reg FD, 5-day move -8.92% suggests capitulation may be overcooked), MANH (UNDERDONE conf 3, re-entry warning active, downgrade to WATCH per rules), DKS (RATIONAL conf 3, skip — symmetric risk, no directional edge).

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector sympathy with Micron-led memory rally; no 8-K, no scheduled earnings
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with SMH +5% and the broader memory/semicap re-rating sparked by Micron commentary on long-term supply agreements. ONTO is leveraged to memory capex (HBM inspection), so it's a logical beneficiary. No company-specific catalyst — magnitude is roughly in line with sector beta given semicap names typically run ~1.2-1.5x SMH on up days.
entries.what_kills: If SMH gives back gains tomorrow and ONTO holds — would suggest a company-specific bid we missed (M&A, contract win)
entries.reasoning: ONTO is flagged as RATIONAL (not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE), confidence 3. Per intake rules, only OVERDONE/UNDERDONE flags with confidence ≥3 are buy-eligible. This flag does not qualify. Substantively: the thesis is pure sector sympathy (SMH +5%, memory rally, ONTO +6.22% up 1.2-1.5x beta). No company-specific catalyst. Five-day move +5.19%, volume 0.86x (below average). The 'what_confirms' section explicitly states the edge is follow-through if peers extend — meaning this is a momentum ride, not a mean-reversion or catalyst play. My track record shows RATIONAL flags are not in my playbook here (instruction set limits entries to OVERDONE/UNDERDONE). Skip.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-04 with items 5.02, 5.03, 5.07, 9.01 — pattern consistent with annual meeting results
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.03 + 5.07 dated 2026-06-04 fit the pattern of an annual meeting filing (5.07 = vote results, 5.03 = charter amendments approved, 5.02 = board reshuffle/director election). That's normally NOT a material negative — it's housekeeping. But on a risk-off day with XBI -3.6%, the market is selling first and reading later. If the 5.02 turns out to be a routine board election rather than a CEO/CSO departure, the magnitude (~3x XBI move) is excessive for a clinical-stage name with no pipeline news.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unplanned CFO/CMO/CEO departure, or 5.03 includes a dilutive authorized-share increase that signals impending raise — either would justify the move and then some
entries.reasoning: This is the same name I just exited from my open position. The re-flag is OVERDONE conf 3, arguing the -8.97% move is excessive for routine annual-meeting 8-K items. Rather than immediately re-buy at current levels, I need clarity on what the 8-K actually says. The flag's 'what_confirms' section states: 'Reading the actual 8-K shows 5.02 is director election results from the annual meeting, not an executive departure; no pipeline/trial news in the exhibits.' If that reads true after market open, the thesis flips back to UNDERDONE and a re-entry becomes justified. But I don't have that confirmation yet. Watch for the 8-K to be read and digested by sell-side commentary in the next 6-12 hours. If Monday morning shows 5.02 is indeed routine board election and peers stabilize, CRSP will likely bounce hard and I can enter at better levels (likely +3-5% from here). If the 8-K reveals an unplanned departure or share authorization, the OVERDONE flag was wrong and the position belongs down here. Either way, waiting 24 hours for clarity is higher-EV than re-entering blind.

entries.name: Five Below, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings release filed via 8-K on 2026-06-03 (item 2.02)
entries.thesis: A sharp double-digit decline on the day of an earnings 8-K, in the context of LULU also cutting guidance and citing undisclosed headwinds, fits a justified earnings disappointment in discretionary retail. The five-day move closely matches today's move, suggesting the drop is the earnings reaction itself rather than an overshoot. Magnitude looks appropriate for a guide-down at a stock that had been priced for continued execution.
entries.what_kills: 8-K reveals a beat with conservative guide that was misread; or a one-time charge (inventory write-down) that's non-recurring, in which case the move would be OVERDONE rather than RATIONAL.
entries.reasoning: FIVE is flagged RATIONAL confidence 4, not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE. Per intake rules, RATIONAL flags are not buy-eligible. Substantively, the -13.78% move and -14.55% five-day move match the same-day earnings reaction closely (no overshoot), and the thesis explicitly states 'Magnitude looks appropriate for a guide-down at a stock that had been priced for continued execution.' That is the definition of a justified move, not a misprice. The flag also notes that when a discretionary retailer prints earnings the same day a major peer issues a guide-cut, 'the move is rarely a fade on day one.' That is arguing FOR the move, not against it — confirming that FIVE's decline is rationally priced and not a candidate for entry. Skip.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-03 with officer departure (5.02) and Reg FD disclosure (7.01)
entries.thesis: ALK has now dropped sharply over five days and added another leg lower on a same-day 8-K disclosing officer departure plus Reg FD. CFO/operational departures mid-quarter at airlines tend to precede guidance issues; combined with the prior week's slide, the market may still be underpricing follow-through if the departure is abrupt rather than planned retirement. Small-cap airline at ~$4.7B with no buffer.
entries.what_kills: Departure is a planned CEO/board retirement with successor in place, or Reg FD item is a routine investor conference deck with no negative content — then today's drop is the capitulation low
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The five-day decline of -8.92% already embeds the market's repricing of officer departure risk, making the 'follow-through' thesis depend on hidden information that would only emerge if the Reg FD disclosure itself reveals it — yet the flag does not confirm the Reg FD content is negative. Bear case: The bull case rests on 'the market may still be underpricing follow-through,' but a -8.92% five-day move in a $4.7B airline on an officer departure is substantial and likely already reflects the visible catalyst. The flag's 'what_kills' criterion explicitly identifies the failure mode: 'Reg FD item is a routine investor conference deck with no negative content — then today's drop is the capitulation low.' The red team cannot access the actual 8-K exhibit, but the flag provides no assertion that the Reg FD disclosed guidance cuts, margin warnings, or other negative forward indicators; it only says the flag 'may' be underpricing it. At 0.96x volume and only -4.65% same-day move despite the filing, institutional interest appears muted, suggesting the market has already digested the news. The buy thesis requires the Reg FD to contain hidden bad news; if it is routine (the baseline case at most filings), the trade is long a capitulation low with poor risk/reward. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: ALK is flagged UNDERDONE conf 3, -4.65% today on a 5-day decline of -8.92%. The setup is officer departure (5.02) + Reg FD (7.01) filed 2026-06-03 on an airline that has already bled 8.92% over five days. The thesis argues the market may still be underpricing follow-through: 'CFO/operational departures mid-quarter at airlines tend to precede guidance issues.' The 'what_confirms' section is specific: 'CFO or COO departure without successor named, or Reg FD disclosure flags soft RASM/fuel-cost guidance; analyst downgrades follow within 48 hours.' The 'what_kills' section is equally clear: 'Departure is a planned CEO/board retirement with successor in place, or Reg FD item is a routine investor conference deck with no negative content — then today's drop is the capitulation low.' This is a named catalyst (8-K URL provided, Reg FD disclosure) with a coherent and testable thesis. The UNDERDONE label at conf 3 + a five-day move of -8.92% + sub-1x volume suggest selling exhaustion rather than capitulation panic. This fits the exploratory tier criteria: real catalyst URL, clear hypothesis (officer departure + Reg FD = guidance warning), and confidence ≥3. The airline sector is under pressure but ALK's specific 5-day move is large enough that a false-positive fade (planned retirement, benign Reg FD) would be a quick mean-reversion win. A true-positive (unplanned departure, hidden guidance cut) would have more room on follow-through. Exploratory sizing (6% = ~$600) caps downside while allowing grading signal if the hypothesis plays out.)

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day decline of -8.92% already embeds the market's repricing of officer departure risk, making the 'follow-through' thesis depend on hidden information that would only emerge if the Reg FD disclosure itself reveals it — yet the flag does not confirm the Reg FD content is negative.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on 'the market may still be underpricing follow-through,' but a -8.92% five-day move in a $4.7B airline on an officer departure is substantial and likely already reflects the visible catalyst. The flag's 'what_kills' criterion explicitly identifies the failure mode: 'Reg FD item is a routine investor conference deck with no negative content — then today's drop is the capitulation low.' The red team cannot access the actual 8-K exhibit, but the flag provides no assertion that the Reg FD disclosed guidance cuts, margin warnings, or other negative forward indicators; it only says the flag 'may' be underpricing it. At 0.96x volume and only -4.65% same-day move despite the filing, institutional interest appears muted, suggesting the market has already digested the news. The buy thesis requires the Reg FD to contain hidden bad news; if it is routine (the baseline case at most filings), the trade is long a capitulation low with poor risk/reward.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-01 with Items 2.05 (restructuring) and 7.01 (Reg FD disclosure)
entries.thesis: MANH filed an Item 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) plus 7.01 (Reg FD) and the stock is up sharply on above-average volume, extending a multi-day rally. Restructurings filed as standalone events (not bundled with bad earnings) are often read as proactive margin/cost discipline by SaaS investors, especially in supply-chain software where peers have been pressured. The 7.01 disclosure likely contains framing/guidance that the market is digesting positively. If management is reaffirming or raising guidance alongside cost takeouts, follow-through is likely as sell-side updates models.
entries.what_kills: Restructuring is actually a response to weakening bookings/RPO trends disclosed in the 7.01; or the move is purely sympathy with the broader software/AI tape (XLK +2.48%) and fades when sector rotates
entries.reasoning: MANH was CLOSED 0 days ago at a realized loss of -5.00% after a 6-day hold (horizon exit). Re-entry warning is active. The new flag is UNDERDONE conf 3, +7.38% move, +16.21% five-day move, 1.33x volume, citing a restructuring 8-K (2.05 + 7.01) read as proactive cost discipline by SaaS investors. To re-buy, I must satisfy BOTH of: (1) confidence ≥ 4, and (2) genuinely new information not known at exit. This flag has conf 3, failing criterion (1) immediately — the execution layer will downgrade any BUY to WATCH regardless. Substantively, the 'new information' would need to be something that did NOT exist 24 hours ago. The flag cites the same restructuring 8-K filed 2026-06-01 (2 days after my horizon exit on 6/6). I did not have access to that 8-K at exit time. However, the re-entry bar is confidence ≥ 4, and this flag is conf 3. That is a hard stop. Even if the restructuring news is genuinely new, conf 3 fails the raised bar for recently-closed names. Watch MANH for confirmation that the 7.01 Reg FD contains reaffirmed/raised guidance (flag's 'what_confirms' section). If sell-side upgrades follow within 48 hours and the stock breaks 16.21% five-day move to the upside, re-entry at conf 3 may become justifiable next week at higher price — the thesis will have graduated to conf 4 by market action. For now: WATCH.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings report 5/27 with separate 8.01 disclosure, compounded by XLY weakness today
entries.thesis: DKS reported on 5/27 (Items 2.02 + 8.01 + 9.01) and the stock is now down meaningfully cumulatively. XLY is -2.22% today, so today's leg is partly sector sympathy on top of an already-soft earnings reaction. The 8.01 catch-all alongside 2.02 sometimes signals a separately disclosed item (Foot Locker integration update is the obvious candidate given recent M&A). Magnitude looks consistent with weak consumer tape + integration overhang — not obviously overdone or underdone.
entries.what_kills: Sharp reversal if the 8.01 disclosure turns out to be benign (e.g., a buyback authorization rather than guidance cut)
entries.reasoning: DKS is flagged RATIONAL conf 3, -4.66% today, -6.18% five-day move. The thesis is pure sector sympathy (XLY -2.22% today) on top of an already-soft earnings reaction from 5/27 (items 2.02 + 8.01 + 9.01). The flag explicitly states: 'Magnitude looks consistent with weak consumer tape + integration overhang — not obviously overdone or underdone.' That is a statement of symmetry, not a directional edge. The flag is classified RATIONAL, not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE, so it does not meet buy eligibility per intake rules. The 'what_kills' section mentions 'Sharp reversal if the 8.01 disclosure turns out to be benign (e.g., a buyback authorization rather than guidance cut)' — meaning the edge here is betting that the mystery 8.01 item is bad news, not good news. That is not a thesis I can size with confidence. Skip.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy/catch-up trade; no company-specific signal
entries.thesis: LSCC was down 5-day before today and is closing the gap with the SMH rally. Move is consistent with short-covering on a low-volatility risk-on day rather than a fundamental catalyst — no 8-K, no earnings scheduled. The fact it underperformed the 5-day window while peers rallied suggests today is mean-reversion within the sector.
entries.what_kills: If it fades intraday tomorrow back to flat vs SMH — confirms one-day technical bounce

entries.name: Entegris, Inc.
entries.catalyst: AI/semi sector unwind; no company-specific catalyst found
entries.thesis: ENTG is high-beta to wafer-fab capex; with SMH down ~9% and ORCL/AVGO/NVDA all down hard on AI spend rerating, a double-digit drop in ENTG is in line with peers (VECO, ONTO, AEIS all down 7-8%). No company-specific 8-K, no earnings within window — this is sector beta, magnitude appropriate.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K appearing late showing a guide cut or customer-specific issue — would reframe this as company-specific and possibly UNDERDONE to the downside

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible drone-subgroup-specific news or growth multiple compression
entries.thesis: AVAV -9% with KTOS -7.7% and MRCY -5.6% — drone/unmanned subgroup is selling off much harder than the broader defense complex (ITA -0.9%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible. Could be growth-multiple compression bleeding from tech into high-multiple defense, or a subgroup-specific news item not captured. Can't articulate a confident direction without knowing which.
entries.what_kills: If the subgroup move is just multiple compression on high-beta defense, it should mean-revert when growth stabilizes — and would be UNDERDONE on the long side

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Defensive rotation; no company-specific signal in catalyst feeds
entries.thesis: TXRH up on a day when XLY is down ~2% is the rotation tell — investors moving into defensive consumer names with steady traffic. No company-specific catalyst visible, but the relative move (TXRH vs. XLY ~8 points of dispersion) is consistent with classic risk-off rotation into staples-adjacent restaurants. Magnitude is appropriate to the regime shift; not a mispricing.
entries.what_kills: An undisclosed analyst upgrade or M&A rumor surfacing — would reframe as idiosyncratic and possibly UNDERDONE

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.thesis: NTLA leads the gene-editing group up double digits on elevated but not extreme volume, with empty catalyst_signals. Without an 8-K, a press release in news flow, or a conference data point I can point to, I cannot determine whether this is a clinical data leak, M&A speculation, or short-covering in a beaten-down name. Both a bull case (real catalyst, follow-through) and a bear case (squeeze fades) are plausible but neither articulates at conf 3+ without more evidence.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses tomorrow with no follow-through and no identified catalyst — confirms squeeze/noise.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.thesis: ITA is up nearly 3% and Kratos, AVAV, MRCY are all up mid-to-high single digits. The counterintuitive part: Trump-reluctant-to-resume-Iran-war should pressure defense, yet the group is bid. More likely explanation is a budget/appropriations catalyst, a specific drone/unmanned program award, or rotation into industrials (XLI +1.2%). With volume actually below average on KTOS and AVAV, this looks like a coordinated rotation/multi-day positioning rather than a news spike. Magnitude appears appropriate to a sector rotation move.
entries.what_kills: Stocks reverse with no identifiable catalyst — would suggest a one-day squeeze rather than rotation.

entries.name: GameStop Corporation
entries.thesis: GME up ~6% on 1.88x volume the day after an earnings 8-K. Cannot tell from filing codes alone whether print was actually good or whether this is retail/meme positioning. GME's price action has been decoupled from fundamentals for years; without reading the actual release, a directional call past 'reacted positively' would be guessing.

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Healthcare/biotech sector strength; 5/29 8-K appears to be annual meeting (5.07) and director election (5.02), not today's driver
entries.thesis: EXEL +6.3% on below-average volume in a strong biotech tape. The 5.02 + 5.07 8-K is dated 5/29 (annual meeting director slate — routine), not today's catalyst. Move likely reflects XBI strength plus a possible clinical/pipeline data point not visible here. Magnitude is in line with quality biotech moving with a hot XBI day.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.thesis: AEIS up sharply but five-day still negative, suggesting today is a recovery within a downtrend driven by the broader semi-cap rally (SMH +4%). Magnitude reasonable given the bounce-back math and beta. No catalyst signals.
entries.what_kills: AEIS-specific announcement (guidance reset, customer concentration disclosure) emerging — would force reclassification

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics

entries.thesis: PTCT down hard but five-day is positive and volume is BELOW average (0.75x). XBI -4.4% explains roughly half the move, leaving a residual that could be idiosyncratic or simply beta-amplified. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no headline visible. Cannot articulate either a bull (oversold bounce) or bear (specific catalyst) case at conf 3+ without knowing what dragged XBI.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: BEAM -7% on a day XBI is -4.4% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap pre-revenue gene editing name. Volume is below average (0.87x), arguing against panic selling. Magnitude appears to match sector pressure rather than name-specific bad news.
entries.what_kills: An overnight 8-K on a pipeline setback or partnership termination would reclassify as UNDERDONE

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources

Could be coking coal price strength, China stimulus chatter, or short squeeze dynamics — none of which I can verify from the data provided. Volume only roughly average, which argues against a fundamental catalyst.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma
entries.thesis: BBIO filed an 8.01 + 9.01 on 5/27 (likely a clinical or commercial update on Attruby/acoramidis) and has drifted down on >2.5x volume into a soft XBI tape (-2.25%). The volume signature suggests material news but the modest price reaction is ambiguous — could be a digestion phase before a larger move either direction. Without reading the 8.01 exhibit, I can't tell if it's a script update (likely market-moving) or routine.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is administrative (e.g., conference presentation slides), making this just XBI sympathy
